name: Additive Manufacturing (3D Printing for Aerospace)
description: Metal additive manufacturing technologies (SLM, EBM, DED) used to 3D-print rocket engine components, reducing part count, lead time, and cost. SpaceX 3D-prints SuperDraco engine chambers, and Relativity Space 3D-prints entire rockets.
whyUseIt: Additive manufacturing lets you print complex internal geometries (cooling channels, injector elements) that are impossible with traditional machining. A rocket engine combustion chamber that required 100 parts with traditional manufacturing can be printed as a single piece.

vocabulary.definition: Selective Laser Melting — a laser fuses metal powder layer by layer to build up a part
A nickel superalloy commonly 3D-printed for rocket engine components — withstands extreme temperatures

beginnerMistakes: Assuming 3D-printed parts have the same properties as wrought material
Not designing for the manufacturing process (support structures, build orientation)
Skipping post-processing heat treatment that is critical for aerospace-grade properties
